title.en: MEASUREMENT OF NUCLEAR OR X-RADIATION (radiation analysis of materials, mass spectrometry G01N23/00; tubes for determining the presence, intensity, density or energy of radiation or particles H01J47/00)
Measuring X-radiation, gamma radiation, corpuscular radiation, or cosmic radiation (G01T3/00, G01T5/00 take precedence)
{Scintillation (flow) cells}
{Total absorption calorimeters; Shower detectors}
Dosimeters (G01T1/15 takes precedence)
Instruments in which pulses generated by a radiation detector are integrated, e.g. by a diode pump circuit
Measuring radiation intensity (G01T1/29 takes precedence {; self-powered detectors G01T3/006; using an ionisation chamber filled with a liquid or solid, e.g. frozen liquid, dielectric G01T3/008})
Measurement performed on radiation beams, e.g. position or section of the beam; Measurement of spatial distribution of radiation
Measuring half-life of a radioactive substance {(period meters for nuclear fission reactors G21C17/14)}
Measuring polarisation of particles
Measuring cross-section, e.g. absorption cross-section of particles

Measuring spectral distribution of X-rays or of nuclear radiation {spectrometry (pulse selection circuits per se H03K; investigation of materials by radiation diffraction G01N23/20; spectrometer tubes H01J49/00)}
Measuring neutron radiation (G01T5/00 takes precedence)
{Spectrometry}

{using self-powered detectors (for neutrons as well as for Y- or X-rays), e.g. using Compton-effect (Compton diodes) or photo-emission or a (n,B) nuclear reaction (radioisotopic generators G21H1/00, e.g. G21H1/02, G21H1/04; photo-tubes H01J40/00; thermionic generators H01J45/00)}
{using an ionisation chamber filled with a gas, liquid or solid, e.g. frozen liquid, dielectric (G01T3/006 takes precedence)}
by shielding other radiation
using calorimetric devices
with scintillation detectors
with semiconductor detectors
Recording of movements or tracks of particles (spark chambers H01J47/00); Processing or analysis of such tracks
{using a combination of several movement of track recording devices}
{Non-electrical readout of multi-wire or parallel-plate chambers (non-electrical readout in such chambers per se H01J47/22)}
Processing of tracks; Analysis of tracks
Cloud chambers, e.g. Wilson chamber
Bubble chambers
Scintillation chambers (discharge tubes H01J40/00, H01J47/00)
Plates or blocks in which tracks of nuclear particles are made visible by after-treatment, e.g. using photographic emulsion, using mica
Circuit arrangements with multi-wire or parallel-plate chambers, e.g. spark chambers (tubes per se H01J47/00)
Details of radiation-measuring instruments
{calibration techniques (stabilization of spectrometer G01T1/40)}

Collecting means for receiving or storing samples to be investigated {and possibly directly transporting the samples to the measuring arrangement; particularly for investigating radioactive fluids (sampling, preparing specimens for investigation in general G01N1/00, G01N1/02; shielded cells or rooms structurally combined with manipulatin devices G21F; measuring of chromatographically separated samples G01N30/00 - G01N30/96)}
Means for conveying samples received
Provision for actuation of an alarm
